rulings.Case Title: GARY WAI SHING WONG et al VS. FIRST CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 29, 2010, line 9, Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. A HEARING IS REQUIRED. GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS PRESENT A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS, AND HAVE DEMONSTRATED IRREPARABLE HARM IN THE FORECLOSURE OF THEIR HOME. PLAINTIFF SHALL POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $80,000 PLUS MAKE MONTHLY PAYMENTS IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DETERMINED AT THE HEARING OF THIS MOTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR H. SUTTER et al VS. GREGORY RYKEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Hearing On Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitrators
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 29, 2010, line 11, PETITIONERS ARTHUR SUTTER, JUDITH PIFER, JUDD KESSLER, AND NORTH AMERICAN RESOURCES COROPORATION Hearing On Petition To Confirm Award Of Arbitrators. DENIED. THE FACTS OF SHIVER ARE DISTINGUISHABLE IN THAT THE PARTIES WERE DIFFERENT AND THE COMPLAINT WAS FOR MALPRACTICE. HERE, THE PARTIES ARE THE SAME AND A COMPARISON OF THE FEE AWARD AND THE FEE ACTION CLEARLY DEMONSTRATE THAT THE SAME FEES ARE INVOLVED. SEE WALKER V. LA CO. METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 15. =(302/CWW)
